mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest PAYEMS value is 150399.0, showing continued job growth.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the 3-month rate of change of CPIAUCSL above a critical threshold signals persistent inflation, forcing monetary tightening expectations that increase discount rates and compress equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) is only bullish for equities when housing starts (HOUST) are also stable or growing. This interaction captures broad-based consumer and investment health, whereas strong retail sales with weak housing signals a fragile consumer.
failure_condition: A model using both RSAFS and HOUST does not show a statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) in predictive power over a model using only RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its 12-month low is a leading indicator of recession. This signal is most potent during late-cycle expansions, as it marks a definitive turn in the labor market, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 3.8, which is low by historical standards but has ticked up.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in at least two of the last three NBER-defined recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a robust manufacturing and supply-side of the economy, which supports corporate revenue growth and leads to positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) and slowing industrial production (falling INDPRO) creates a stagflationary environment. This interaction is highly negative for equities as it squeezes corporate margins and prompts restrictive monetary policy into a weakening economy.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change CPIAUCSL > 3% AND Year-over-year % change INDPRO < 1%
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative returns) falls below 60% during periods that satisfy the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden, sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals an abrupt tightening of financial conditions and a collapse in investment sentiment, causing a rapid, negative repricing of equity risk over a short horizon.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the average 5-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is near the zero lower bound, accommodative monetary policy supports equity valuations by lowering the discount rate and encouraging risk-taking ('TINA' - There Is No Alternative).
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.08, which is near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not statistically higher than the average return when FEDFUNDS > 0.5%.
